facts.context: The tightest possible bound on 3D enstrophy growth rate dOmega/dt in terms of known quantities is:
facts.truth: dOmega/dt <= C * Omega^(3/2) * E^(-1/2) — enstrophy growth is controlled by the ratio of enstrophy to energy, giving a conditional regularity criterion
distractors: dOmega/dt <= C * Omega * ||omega||_infinity — linear in enstrophy times the vorticity maximum, reproducing the BKM condition
dOmega/dt <= C * Omega^2 / E — quadratic in enstrophy divided by energy, implying blowup only when enstrophy vastly exceeds energy
dOmega/dt <= C * P^(2/3) * Omega^(1/3) — involving palinstrophy, connecting enstrophy growth to its own dissipation rate
